content.problemStatement:
You are given a K√óKK \times KK√óK matrix MMM. For each rrr and ccc (1‚Č§r,c‚Č§K1 \le r, c \le K1‚Č§r,c‚Č§K), let's denote the element in the rrr-th row and ccc-th column by Mr,cM_{r, c}Mr,c‚Äč. You are also given three integers NNN, aaa and ddd. Let's define a K√óKK \times KK√óK matrix
S=‚ąĎi=0NFa+i‚čÖd‚čÖMi‚ÄČ.S = \sum_{i=0}^N F_{a + i \cdot d} \cdot M^i \,.S=‚ąĎi=0N‚ÄčFa+i‚čÖd‚Äč‚čÖMi.
Here, M0M^0M0 is the identity matrix and for any non-negative integer iii, FiF_iFi‚Äč is the iii-th Fibonacci number ‚Äē formally, F0=0F_0 = 0F0‚Äč=0, F1=1F_1 = 1F1‚Äč=1 and Fi=Fi‚ąí1+Fi‚ąí2F_i = F_{i-1} + F_{i-2}Fi‚Äč=Fi‚ąí1‚Äč+Fi‚ąí2‚Äč for i>1i \gt 1i>1.

Let's denote elements of the matrix SSS in the same way as for MMM. For each valid rrr and ccc, compute Sr,cS_{r, c}Sr,c‚Äč modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: 1 ‚Č§ K ‚Č§ 100 ( ő£K over all test cases ‚Č§ 100 )
0 ‚Č§ N ‚Č§ 10^{1000} (N is given as a decimal string)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains four space‚ÄĎseparated integers K, a, d and N. Then K lines follow, each containing K integers M_{i,1} ‚Ä¶ M_{i,K} (0 ‚Č§ M_{i,j} < 998244353).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output K lines. The i‚ÄĎth line contains K space‚ÄĎseparated integers S_{i,1} ‚Ä¶ S_{i,K}, where S = ő£_{i=0}^{N} F_{a+i¬∑d}¬∑M^{i} and all values are taken modulo 998244353.

solution.approach: The term F_{a+i¬∑d} can be expressed with the 2√ó2 Fibonacci Q‚ÄĎmatrix Q = [[1,1],[1,0]]: F_n = (Q^n)[0][1]. Let A = Q^a and D = Q^d. Then F_{a+i¬∑d} = (A¬∑D^i)[0][1]. The required sum is S = ő£_{i=0}^{N} (e0^T¬∑A¬∑D^i¬∑e1)¬∑M^i, where e0 = [1,0]^T and e1 = [0,1]^T. We compute three sequences for a length L = N+1 (the number of terms):   ‚ÄĘ P(L) = M^L,   ‚ÄĘ Q(L) = D^L,   ‚ÄĘ S(L) = ő£_{i=0}^{L-1} (e0^T¬∑A¬∑D^i¬∑e1)¬∑M^i.   Using binary exponentiation on the binary representation of L we can combine two halves in O(log L) steps:   If we already know (S, P, Q) for length len, then for length 2¬∑len:   ‚ÄĘ S(2¬∑len) = S(len) + P(len)¬∑(e0^T¬∑A¬∑Q(len)¬∑e1)¬∑S(len)   ‚ÄĘ P(2¬∑len) = P(len)¬∑P(len)   ‚ÄĘ Q(2¬∑len) = Q(len)¬∑Q(len)   The scalar (e0^T¬∑A¬∑Q(len)¬∑e1) is obtained by multiplying the 2√ó2 matrices A and Q(len) and taking element (0,1). When processing a bit equal to 1 we additionally append one more term using the current powers. All matrix multiplications are performed modulo MOD.   Because N can have up to 1000 decimal digits we treat it as a big integer string and repeatedly divide by 2 to obtain its binary representation (O(|N|¬∑log N) ‚Č§ 10^4 operations, easily fast).   The overall complexity per test case is O(K^3¬∑log N) (K ‚Č§ 100, log N ‚Č§ 3500), well within limits. Memory usage is O(K^2).
solution.timeComplexity: O(K^3 ¬∑ log N) per test case
